content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers. You have to answer QQQ queries of the following two types:



111 LLL RRR (R>L)(R > L)(R>L) which asks you to find gcd⁡(AL,lcm(AL+1,gcd⁡(AL+2,...,((R−L) mod 2==1?\gcd(A_L, lcm(A_{L + 1}, \gcd(A_{L + 2}, ... , ((R - L) \bmod 2 == 1 ? gcd(AL​,lcm(AL+1​,gcd(AL+2​,...,((R−L)mod2==1? gcd⁡(AR−1,AR):lcm(AR−1,AR))…)))\gcd(A_{R - 1}, A_{R}) : lcm(A_{R - 1}, A_{R}))\ldots)))gcd(AR−1​,AR​):lcm(AR−1​,AR​))…))).




222 LLL RRR (R>L)(R > L)(R>L) which asks you to find the same as above but lcmlcmlcm swapped with gcd⁡\gcdgcd and vice-versa.



Here lcm(a,b)lcm(a, b)lcm(a,b) and gcd⁡(a,b)\gcd(a, b)gcd(a,b) denotes the least common multiple and greatest common divisor of two integers aaa and bbb respectively.

**Example:** Consider the array AAA = [2,4,8,16,32,64][2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64][2,4,8,16,32,64].



Suppose a query is of the form 111 111 666, so it asks you to find:
gcd⁡(2,lcm(4,gcd⁡(8,lcm(16,gcd⁡(32,64)))))\gcd(2, lcm(4, \gcd(8, lcm(16, \gcd(32, 64)))))gcd(2,lcm(4,gcd(8,lcm(16,gcd(32,64))))).




Suppose a query is of the form 222 111 555, so it asks you to find:
lcm(2,gcd⁡(4,lcm(8,gcd⁡(16,32))))lcm(2, \gcd(4, lcm(8, \gcd(16, 32))))lcm(2,gcd(4,lcm(8,gcd(16,32)))).



**Note:** Since input-output is large, prefer using fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase contains Q+2Q + 2Q+2 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers, NNN and QQQ.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains three space-separated integers Typei,Li,RiType_i, L_i, R_iTypei​,Li​,Ri​, denoting the type and parameters of the ithi^{th}ith query.


Output Format
For each query, output in a single line answer to the problem.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 630630630.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The answer for the first query is  =gcd⁡(2,lcm(4,gcd⁡(8,lcm(16,gcd⁡(32,64)))))= \gcd(2, lcm(4, \gcd(8, lcm(16, \gcd(32, 64)))))=gcd(2,lcm(4,gcd(8,lcm(16,gcd(32,64))))) === gcd⁡(2,lcm(4,gcd⁡(8,lcm(16,32))))\gcd(2, lcm(4, \gcd(8, lcm(16, 32))))gcd(2,lcm(4,gcd(8,lcm(16,32)))) = gcd⁡(2,lcm(4,gcd⁡(8,32)))\gcd(2, lcm(4, \gcd(8, 32)))gcd(2,lcm(4,gcd(8,32))) = gcd⁡(2,lcm(4,8))\gcd(2, lcm(4, 8))gcd(2,lcm(4,8)) = gcd⁡(2,8)\gcd(2, 8)gcd(2,8) = 222.
The answer for the second query is =lcm(2,gcd⁡(4,lcm(8,gcd⁡(16,32))))= lcm(2, \gcd(4, lcm(8, \gcd(16, 32))))=lcm(2,gcd(4,lcm(8,gcd(16,32)))) = lcm(2,gcd⁡(4,lcm(8,16)))lcm(2, \gcd(4, lcm(8, 16)))lcm(2,gcd(4,lcm(8,16))) = lcm(2,gcd⁡(4,16))lcm(2, \gcd(4, 16))lcm(2,gcd(4,16)) = lcm(2,4)lcm(2, 4)lcm(2,4) = 444.
